label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out the main idea of the passage. The question gives me four options: A, B, C, D. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand what it's about. The first paragraph talks about London's subway system and its historical value, mentioning that it's a must for visitors. But then the next paragraphs start discussing how people in London use their time on the subway. It says they avoid eye contact and are busy with their own things. The focus shifts to technology—like iPhones, apps, iPods, and even palm DVD technology. It lists several apps and how they're used by commuters. The passage ends by saying that with all these distractions, it's amazing people remember to get off the train. So, the main idea isn't just about the subway's historical significance (which is mentioned in the first paragraph) but more about how technology is changing the way commuters use their time on the subway. The author uses examples of various apps and devices that people use to pass the time, which points towards technological changes affecting commuting habits. Looking at the options: A) London commuters are unfriendly to strangers. The passage mentions avoiding eye contact, but it clarifies that it's not rudeness, they're just busy. So this isn't the main idea. B) Apple has earned a lot from 40,000 apps. While the passage does mention Apple and the number of apps, it's an example to support the broader point about technology changing commuting habits, not the main idea itself. C) Technology is changing how London commuters spend their traveling time. This seems to align with the passage's focus on apps, iPods, and other tech being used on the subway. D) Riding a London subway is a must when visiting London. The first paragraph mentions this, but the rest of the passage doesn't focus on promoting the subway as a tourist attraction; it focuses more on the behavior and tech usage of commuters.
